scope_boundary: deterministic generation-mode and resource-budget fixture accounting only; no generation speed, speculative decoding quality, diffusion quality, KV-cache throughput, routing quality, model-quality, useful-solution-per-second performance, or runtime behavior result
transition_reason: The repository now has a deterministic generation-mode baseline harness with valid and expected-invalid fixtures over generation-mode and resource-budget records. It catches missing baseline and negative-control refs, latency-only proxy acceptance, failed-quality promotion, missing fallback behavior for medium-or-higher non-autoregressive modes, resource-budget mismatch, and support-state promotion attempts. External fast-generation source notes also cover speculative decoding, multi-token prediction, Medusa, EAGLE, lookahead decoding, early exit, PagedAttention, state-space alternatives, and diffusion language-model families. These artifacts improve the accounting discipline for comparing generation modes, but they do not run a model, reproduce a paper result, measure speed-quality curves, establish useful-solution-per-second performance, or validate runtime routing.
required_artifacts: autoregressive baseline run with task set, quality metric, residual accounting, and environment record
speculative decoding or draft-head run with acceptance, rejection, repair, and quality accounting
diffusion or non-autoregressive generation run with matched baseline and negative control
KV-cache or serving-throughput measurement with memory pressure and quality boundary
router-selected generation-mode trace with fallback and risk-tier enforcement
independent review of source interpretation adequacy
accepted upward evidence-transition review for any narrowed stronger claim
negative_results: generation-mode baseline fixtures are deterministic accounting fixtures
no model inference benchmark was run
no speculative decoding, diffusion, early-exit, state-space, or KV-cache throughput result was reproduced
no useful-solution-per-second performance claim is established
downgrade_triggers: chapter text interpreted as a measured speed or quality result
latency-only proxy used as a useful-solution-per-second claim
promotion_burden: Move above argument only after the claim is narrowed to deterministic accounting behavior that passed, or after matched model runs, baseline and negative-control records, quality/residual measurements, fallback traces, and independent source-interpretation review are recorded with accepted evidence-transition records.
limitations: deterministic fixtures do not run an LLM or generation system
schema validation does not prove acceptance quality or runtime routing
external source notes provide comparison vocabulary rather than reproduced results
useful-solution-per-second remains a target metric, not a measured local result
no independent model-evaluation or source-interpretation review has been recorded
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; not an independent model-evaluation, benchmarking, or source-interpretation audit
acceptance_blockers: no speculative decoding or diffusion run
non_claims: does not promote Fast Generation above argument
does not prove runtime routing or deployment behavior
